entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop that is susceptible to aflatoxin contamination.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of toxic aflatoxins in groundnuts and their products.

entities.name: CULTURAL PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural methods used during the cultivation of crops to manage contamination.

entities.name: PRODUCE HANDLING
entities.description: The methods used to manage groundnuts after harvest to prevent contamination.

entities.name: STORAGE CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environmental factors during storage that affect aflatoxin levels.

entities.name: GENETIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherited ability of groundnuts to resist fungal invasion and toxin production.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS AVUS
entities.description: A fungal species that invades groundnut seeds and produces aflatoxins.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN PRODUCTION
entities.description: The biological process of synthesizing aflatoxins by fungi.

entities.name: SEED INVASION
entities.description: The process and state of fungal penetration into the groundnut seed.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, an organization focusing on agricultural research.

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions characterized by a high prevalence of small-scale groundnut farmers.
